source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Autonomous loop iteration limit reached
notes: Spec asserts the loop's termination shape: working → ready even when the goal isn't met, state doesn't stick in working. Claudecode's /goal terminates via three paths that share the same end_turn → ready signal: goal_status met:true (natural), /goal clear (user-driven), or max_iterations cap (hard-coded). The recipe uses /goal clear after multiple iterations as the stand-in for cap-hit because the cap isn't deterministically forceable today; the state arc is identical. The strict invariant on goal_cleared is that the session never enters waiting (no permission gates) and that after the final iteration ends, the state stays ready — no working flicker that would indicate a stuck loop.

text: /goal <unsatisfiable> drives the first iteration ready → working
First iteration ends. Stop-hook re-prompts because the unsatisfiable goal can't be met.
Second iteration begins under Stop-hook control — agent keeps trying because the goal condition is impossible to satisfy
Second iteration ends. The agent has not declared goal_status met:true (the condition is impossible) — the loop continues.

Spec: 'state reaches ready even though the agent didn't fully succeed' — after /goal clear (the stand-in for max_iterations being hit), the loop terminates. State stays ready. Validator allows another working blip from a last-in-flight iteration completing before /goal clear takes effect (the no-working invariant is intentionally omitted; the cap-hit / clear path leaves a single final ready that anchors here).
